title: Impedance assembly
abstract: Impedance assembly (120) for use in a voltage divider for sensing an AC elevated voltage of at least 1 kV of a power-carrying conductor (10) distributing electrical energy in a national grid. The impedance assembly comprises a) a PCB (170); b) a high-voltage contact (80) for connection to the power-carrying conductor; c)a first plurality of impedance elements (70) on the PCB, connected to the high-voltage contact and in series with each other such as to be operable in a first voltage divider (20) for sensing the voltage of the power-carrying conductor; and d) a second plurality of impedance elements (71) on the PCB, connected to the high-voltage contact and in series with each other such as to be operable in a second voltage divider (21) for harvesting electrical energy from the power-carrying conductor.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. Impedance assembly (120, 122, 123, 124) for use in a voltage divider (20) for sensing an AC elevated voltage of at least 1 kV versus ground of a power-carrying conductor (10) distributing electrical energy in a national grid, wherein the impedance assembly comprises a) a printed circuit board (170, 171); b) an externally accessible high-voltage contact (80) for connection to the power carrying conductor; c) a first plurality of impedance elements (70), arranged on the printed circuit board and electrically connected to the high-voltage contact and in series with each other such as to be operable in a first voltage divider (20) for sensing the elevated voltage of the power-carrying conductor; characterized in that the impedance assembly further comprises d) a second plurality of impedance elements (71), arranged on the printed circuit board and electrically connected to the high-voltage contact and in series with each other such as to be operable in a second voltage divider (21) for harvesting electrical energy from the power-carrying conductor. 2. Impedance assembly according to claim 1, wherein the printed circuit board (170, 171) has a first major outer surface (180) and an opposed second major outer surface (190), and wherein the first plurality of impedance elements (70) is arranged on the first major outer surface, and the second plurality of impedance elements (71) is arranged on the second major outer surface. 3. Impedance assembly according to claim 2, wherein a first impedance element (70a) of the first plurality of impedance elements (70) is arranged at a first position on the first major outer surface (180), and a first impedance element (7la) of the second plurality of impedance elements (71) is arranged at the same first position on the second major outer surface (190); and wherein a second impedance element (70b) of the first plurality of impedance elements (70) is arranged at a second position on the first major outer surface (180), and a second impedance element (7lb) of the second plurality of impedance elements (71) is arranged at the same second position on the second major outer surface (190). 4. Impedance assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first plurality of impedance elements (70) is arranged to form a straight row of impedance elements or two straight rows of impedance elements. 5. Impedance assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein each impedance element (70) of the first plurality of impedance elements (70) has a same first nominal impedance, and/or each impedance element (71) of the second plurality of impedance elements (71) has a same second nominal impedance. 6. Impedance assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein each impedance element (70) of the first plurality of impedance elements (70) is a discrete, surface- mounted capacitor, and/or each impedance element (71) of the second plurality of impedance elements (71) is a discrete, surface-mounted capacitor. 7. Impedance assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first plurality of impedance elements (70) comprises more than twenty impedance elements (70), and/or wherein the second plurality of impedance elements (71) comprises more than twenty impedance elements (71). 8. Impedance assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a signal contact (60), connected to the first plurality of impedance elements (70) such as to provide a signal voltage that varies proportionally with the elevated voltage of the power-carrying conductor (10). 9. Impedance assembly according to claim 8, further comprising an interface connector (340) for mechanical and electrical mating with a corresponding plug, wherein the interface connector comprises a plurality of connector contacts (330) for transmitting electrical signals into the plug, and wherein one of the connector contacts (330) is electrically connected to the signal contact (60). 10. Impedance assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, at least a portion of which is encapsulated in a solid, electrically insulating encapsulation body (290). 11. Impedance assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the respective impedances of the second plurality of impedance elements (70) are chosen such that 

 the second voltage divider (21) can harvest electrical energy of at least 50 milliwatt from the power-carrying conductor (10). 12. Impedance assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the second plurality of impedance elements (70) is comprised in an inductor-less step-down converter to convert the elevated voltage of the power-carrying conductor (10) to a voltage of 400 Volt or less. 13. Impedance assembly according to claim 12, wherein the inductor-less step-down converter converts the positive and the negative half-wave of the elevated AC voltage of the power-carrying conductor (10) to a voltage of 400 Volt or less. 14. Sensing assembly comprising a) an impedance assembly (120, 122, 124) according to claim 8, and b) a processing unit (400, 401), electrically connected to the signal contact (60) and powered by electrical energy harvested by the second voltage divider (21), for processing the signal voltage and sensing the AC elevated voltage versus ground of the power- carrying conductor (10). 15. Sensing assembly according to claim 14, wherein the processing unit (400, 401) comprises an antenna (390) for wirelessly transmitting a signal indicative of the AC elevated voltage versus ground of the power-carrying conductor (10). 16. Sensing assembly according to claim 15, wherein the processing unit (400, 401) comprises a Bluetooth interface for wirelessly transmitting the signal according to a protocol described in part 5 of standard 60870 of the International Electrotechnical Commission IEC, as in force on 26 April 2019. 17. Sensing assembly according to one of claims 14, 15 or 16, further comprising c) a current sensor (410), connected to the processing unit (400, 401), for sensing a current through the power-carrying conductor (10), and d) a housing (430) containing a component of the current sensor, wherein the processing unit is arranged in the housing. 18. Power network for distributing electrical energy in a national grid, comprising an impedance assembly (120, 122, 124) according to any one of claims 1 to 13 or a sensing assembly according to any one of claims 14 to 17.
